page: Amazon Web Services g5.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance features an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.3 GHz clock speed. The hardware configuration includes 64 GB of DDR4 memory, a 600 GB local NVMe SSD, and a dedicated NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Network connectivity is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated operations. Synthetic CPU tests, such as single-core stress-ng, place the processor in the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks fall into average and weak categories respectively. Conversely, the server excels in machine learning benchmarks, achieving top-tier results in the top 10% for LLM inference prompt processing and text generation using small and medium models, as well as strong top 25% performance for large model prompt processing.

Given this performance profile, the g5.4xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that can actively leverage the NVIDIA A10G GPU and local NVMe storage. It is less optimal for general-purpose CPU-bound tasks or high-throughput memory operations. Recommended workloads include low-to-medium parameter LLM inference, ray tracing, image processing, and graphics rendering.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR4 memory, 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a single NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. While synthetic CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks place the instance in the average to weak categories, its machine learning capabilities are highly competitive. Specifically, the server achieves top-tier performance in LLM inference for small and medium models, and strong performance for large model prompt processing. This hardware profile makes the g5.4xlarge highly cost-efficient for GPU-driven workloads, including low-to-medium parameter LLM deployment, ray tracing, and graphics rendering, whereas it presents tradeoffs for pure CPU-bound or high-memory-bandwidth applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring an AMD EPYC CPU, 64 GB RAM, 600 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA A10G GPU, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA A10G GPU for machine learning and graphics.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring dedicated NVMe storage and an NVIDIA Ampere GPU, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB VRAM for accelerated computing
AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB DDR4 RAM
600 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier benchmark performance for small and medium LLM inference
Suited for machine learning, ray tracing, and graphics rendering
